_source: GAAP standards, CPA exam materials, QuickBooks certification guides
_note: Long-tail accounting knowledge. Journal entries, reconciliation, financial statement preparation, tax considerations.

double_entry_bookkeeping.acronym: DEALER - Debits: Expenses, Assets, Losses increase; Credits: Equity, Revenue increase

revenue_recognition.cash_basis: DR Cash $1000, CR Revenue $1000 (when cash received)
revenue_recognition.accrual_basis: DR Accounts Receivable $1000, CR Revenue $1000 (when earned); then DR Cash $1000, CR AR $1000 (when collected)
revenue_recognition.unearned_revenue: DR Cash $1200, CR Unearned Revenue $1200 (prepayment); then DR Unearned Revenue $100, CR Revenue $100 (monthly recognition)

expense_recognition.immediate: DR Office Supplies Expense $500, CR Cash $500
expense_recognition.prepaid: DR Prepaid Insurance $1200, CR Cash $1200; then monthly: DR Insurance Expense $100, CR Prepaid Insurance $100
expense_recognition.accrued: DR Wages Expense $5000, CR Wages Payable $5000 (earned but not paid); then DR Wages Payable $5000, CR Cash $5000 (payment)

depreciation.straight_line: DR Depreciation Expense $1000, CR Accumulated Depreciation $1000
depreciation.calculation: (Cost - Salvage Value) / Useful Life; $10,000 asset, $1,000 salvage, 10 years = $900/year
depreciation.contra_account: Accumulated Depreciation reduces asset value; not direct credit to asset

inventory.sale_perpetual: DR AR $8000, CR Sales $8000; DR COGS $5000, CR Inventory $5000 (two entries)
inventory.sale_periodic: DR AR $8000, CR Sales $8000 (only one entry; inventory counted at period end)

payroll.gross_to_net: DR Wages Expense $10,000, CR Wages Payable $7,500, CR Payroll Taxes Payable $2,500
payroll.employer_taxes: DR Payroll Tax Expense $1,500, CR FICA/Medicare/UI Payable $1,500
payroll.payment: DR Wages Payable $7,500, CR Cash $7,500; DR Payroll Taxes Payable $4,000, CR Cash $4,000

bank_reconciliation.purpose: Match bank statement to cash account in ledger; identify errors and timing differences

book_side_adjustments.nsf_check: Non-sufficient funds; customer check bounced; DR AR, CR Cash
book_side_adjustments.bank_fees: Service charges not recorded; DR Bank Fees Expense, CR Cash
book_side_adjustments.interest_earned: Bank interest not recorded; DR Cash, CR Interest Income
book_side_adjustments.errors: Transposition error in recording; $450 recorded as $540; correct entry needed

bank_side_reconciling_items.deposits_in_transit: Deposited but not on bank statement; add to bank balance; no journal entry
bank_side_reconciling_items.outstanding_checks: Written but not cashed; subtract from bank balance; no journal entry
bank_side_reconciling_items.bank_errors: Bank mistake; contact bank; no journal entry unless bank won't correct

bank_reconciliation.formula: Bank Balance + Deposits in Transit - Outstanding Checks ± Bank Errors = Adjusted Bank Balance = Adjusted Book Balance

income_statement.format: Revenue - COGS = Gross Profit - Operating Expenses = Operating Income ± Other Income/Expense = Net Income
income_statement.cogs: Beginning Inventory + Purchases - Ending Inventory (for merchandising)
income_statement.other: Interest Expense, Gain on Sale of Asset
income_statement.multi_step_vs_single_step: Multi-step separates operating from non-operating; single-step groups all revenue and expenses

balance_sheet.format: Assets = Liabilities + Equity (at specific point in time)
balance_sheet.current_assets: Cash, AR, Inventory, Prepaid (<1 year); liquidity order
balance_sheet.fixed_assets: PP&E (Property, Plant, Equipment); at cost - accumulated depreciation
balance_sheet.current_liabilities: AP, Accrued Expenses, Unearned Revenue, Current Portion LT Debt (<1 year)
balance_sheet.long_term_liabilities: Loans, Bonds Payable, Deferred Tax (>1 year)
balance_sheet.equity: Common Stock, Additional Paid-In Capital, Retained Earnings, Treasury Stock

cash_flow_statement.operating_activities: Net income adjusted for non-cash (depreciation) and changes in working capital (AR, Inventory, AP)
cash_flow_statement.investing_activities: Purchase/sale of PP&E, investments, other long-term assets
cash_flow_statement.financing_activities: Borrowing/repaying debt, issuing stock, paying dividends
cash_flow_statement.indirect_method: Start with net income; add back depreciation; subtract increase in AR; add increase in AP
cash_flow_statement.direct_method: Cash receipts from customers - cash paid to suppliers - cash paid for expenses

retained_earnings.formula: Beginning RE + Net Income - Dividends = Ending RE
retained_earnings.links_statements: Income statement net income flows to retained earnings; retained earnings on balance sheet

fifo.name: First-In, First-Out
fifo.effect_rising_prices: Lower COGS, higher net income, higher ending inventory value, higher taxes
fifo.use: Perishables, most businesses; matches physical flow

lifo.name: Last-In, First-Out
lifo.effect_rising_prices: Higher COGS, lower net income, lower taxes, lower ending inventory value
lifo.use: US only; tax benefit during inflation; not allowed under IFRS
lifo.lifo_reserve: Difference between FIFO and LIFO inventory value; disclosed in notes

weighted_average.calculation: Total Cost / Total Units; recalculated after each purchase (perpetual) or at period end (periodic)
weighted_average.effect: Between FIFO and LIFO; smooths price fluctuations

specific_identification.use: Unique expensive items (cars, jewelry, real estate)
specific_identification.tracking: Each item individually tracked; actual cost of sold item used for COGS

allowance_method.purpose: Estimate uncollectible accounts; match expense to revenue period (accrual)
allowance_method.initial_entry: DR Bad Debt Expense $5000, CR Allowance for Doubtful Accounts $5000
allowance_method.write_off: DR Allowance $1000, CR AR $1000 (when specific account deemed uncollectible)
allowance_method.recovery: DR AR $1000, CR Allowance $1000; DR Cash $1000, CR AR $1000 (if later collected)

estimation_methods.percentage_of_sales: Credit Sales × % (e.g., 2%); focuses on income statement matching
estimation_methods.aging_of_receivables: Current (1%), 1-30 days (5%), 31-60 days (10%), 61-90 days (25%), >90 days (50%); focuses on balance sheet accuracy

capitalization_vs_expense.capitalize: Adds future value; extends useful life; improves beyond original condition; >$2500 (varies by policy)
capitalization_vs_expense.examples_capitalize: New roof, building addition, major equipment overhaul

depreciation_methods.straight_line: Equal expense each year; (Cost - Salvage) / Life
depreciation_methods.double_declining_balance: Accelerated; 2 × (1/Life) × Book Value; ignore salvage until final year
depreciation_methods.units_of_production: (Cost - Salvage) × (Units This Period / Total Expected Units); matches usage
depreciation_methods.macrs: Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System; tax depreciation (US); different from book depreciation

liquidity.current_ratio: Current Assets / Current Liabilities; >2.0 is strong; <1.0 is concerning
liquidity.quick_ratio: (Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities; >1.0 is good; acid test
liquidity.working_capital: Current Assets - Current Liabilities; absolute amount, not ratio

profitability.gross_margin: (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue; varies by industry; retail 20-40%, software 70-90%
profitability.net_margin: Net Income / Revenue; 5-10% is typical; <0% is loss
profitability.roa: Net Income / Total Assets; measures asset efficiency; 5-10% is good
profitability.roe: Net Income / Shareholders' Equity; measures return to owners; 15-20% is strong

leverage.debt_to_equity: Total Liabilities / Total Equity; <1.0 is conservative; >2.0 is aggressive
leverage.debt_to_assets: Total Liabilities / Total Assets; >0.6 is highly leveraged
leverage.interest_coverage: Operating Income / Interest Expense; >3.0 is safe; <1.5 is risky

efficiency.inventory_turnover: COGS / Average Inventory; 6-12x typical retail; higher is better (less tied up)
efficiency.receivables_turnover: Credit Sales / Average AR; higher is better (faster collection)
efficiency.days_sales_outstanding: 365 / AR Turnover; 30-45 days typical; >60 days concerning

business_entities.sole_proprietorship: Schedule C; self-employment tax; QBI deduction; simple but no liability protection
business_entities.s_corporation: Pass-through; avoid SE tax on distributions; must pay reasonable salary; <100 shareholders
business_entities.c_corporation: Double taxation (corporate + dividend); can retain earnings; unlimited shareholders; venture-backed
business_entities.llc: Flexible; can elect S-corp or partnership taxation; liability protection; state-level entity

deductions.ordinary_and_necessary: IRS requirement; reasonable amount; directly related to business
deductions.home_office: Exclusive and regular use; simplified method $5/sq ft up to 300 sq ft; or actual expense method
deductions.vehicle: Standard mileage ($0.67/mile 2024) or actual expenses; keep mileage log; business % only
deductions.meals_entertainment: 50% deductible (meals); 100% if company event for all employees; 0% pure entertainment
